canonicalDefinitions.commit stage: The fast feedback stage that validates buildability and baseline quality on pull requests and merge-queue candidates.
canonicalDefinitions.acceptance stage: The stage that verifies release readiness by running automated acceptance tests and then fanning out integration testing and staging rehearsal in parallel.
canonicalDefinitions.integration testing: Automated tests that validate cross-component behavior and contracts in a realistic runtime path.
canonicalDefinitions.staging: A production-like rehearsal environment used to validate deployment and smoke behavior before promotion.
canonicalDefinitions.production promotion: The act of promoting an already tested release candidate to production without rebuilding artifacts.
canonicalDefinitions.merge queue: The trunk integration mechanism that validates required checks on merge-group candidates before writing to main.
canonicalDefinitions.merge_group: The GitHub Actions event used to run required checks for merge-queue candidates.
canonicalDefinitions.release candidate: A specific immutable build/version produced by the pipeline and promoted through later stages without rebuilding.
canonicalDefinitions.cloud deployment pipeline: The deployment pipeline path where production-destined cloud change is validated and promoted stage-by-stage.
canonicalDefinitions.cloud deployment pipeline replay: A manual replay path that reruns automated acceptance test gate and deployment-stage verification for an existing release candidate SHA without rebuilding.
canonicalDefinitions.automated acceptance test gate: The gate that proves the system meets acceptance criteria; failures stop promotion toward release.
canonicalDefinitions.deployment stage: The stage that performs technical deployment and post-deployment verification for an accepted release candidate.
canonicalDefinitions.releasable: The state where software can be released safely at any time.
canonicalDefinitions.release candidate sha: The commit SHA that identifies the exact release candidate being promoted through the pipeline.
canonicalDefinitions.deployment required: A decision flag indicating whether deployment-stage mutation must run for this release candidate.
canonicalDefinitions.deployment pipeline config: Configuration that defines deployment pipeline stages, gates, policies, and promotion rules.
canonicalDefinitions.commit test suite: A fast fail-first test suite (mostly unit tests plus minimal smoke checks) used in the commit stage/check-in gate.
canonicalDefinitions.post deployment verification gate: A verification gate run after deployment to confirm expected production behavior before release.
canonicalDefinitions.release candidate *: The canonical env/output prefix for release-candidate values.
canonicalDefinitions.desktop deployment pipeline: The desktop deployment pipeline path that promotes immutable release candidates through acceptance and deployment-stage gates.
canonicalDefinitions.desktop deployment pipeline yml: Canonical desktop deployment workflow file name.
